update_note: 2026-07-20: GitHub issue #335 round 12 review of both include/ufsecp/ header diffs that tripped the SENSITIVE_PREFIXES changed-file policy. include/ufsecp/ufsecp_version.h.in's UFSECP_DEPRECATED macro + Windows dllexport/static-lib branch-order fix (round 11) is a pure macro/build-config change with zero API behavioral surface -- no existing entry required updating for it. include/ufsecp/ufsecp_gpu.h (an unrelated, already-dirty concurrent task, not part of issue #335) added a new SECRET-BEARING function, ufsecp_gpu_bip352_scan_batch_multispend (scan_privkey32 uploaded to device memory across CUDA/OpenCL/Metal per its own header comment; already exercised by test_gpu_bip352_scan.cpp::test_bip352_multispend_gpu and test_exploit_gpu_bip352_multispend_failclosed.cpp) with no prior entry in this file -- added one below (critical, ct-required). The same diff's ufsecp_gpu_set_metal_shader_path (shader-library path override; validates absolute path + rejects a literal '..' path component; touches no secret material) was reviewed and does not warrant its own entry -- it is a loader-path configuration function, not a crypto operation. Verified: ci/check_api_contracts.py --json reports 0 issues after this update. 2026-06-29: re-attested API security contracts after libbitcoin direct integration and CAAS fast-gate remediation; ci/check_api_contracts.py validates the authored entries and changed-file policy, with no schema or contract drift. 2026-06-12: dev_bug_scanner added SECRET_TABLE_INDEX, UNALIGNED_WORD_LOAD, and OUTPUT_FAIL_OPEN gates. The pass closed fail-open output behavior in secp256k1_ec_pubkey_parse and BCHN secp256k1_schnorr_sign, replaced Schnorr zero-output word-casts with byte checks, and removed CUDA ZK byte-buffer word casts. Evidence: QUALITY:dev_bug_scanner_deep, secp256k1_shim_test pubkey parse failure clearing, bchn_schnorr_fail_clear, run_code_quality no-regression. 2026-06-04: PASS3-SHIM-001 — the ECDSA signature parse functions (shim secp256k1_ecdsa_signature_parse_compact / _parse_der and recoverable_signature_parse_compact) now zero the opaque output sig struct on ANY parse failure (fail-closed, matching upstream libsecp256k1 memset-on-failure; sig bytes are public data → VT path). Evidence: regression_shim_seckey_erase (RT-02), shim_der_zero_r. P2-CT-001 — bip32_derive_path now secure_erases the intermediate child private key + chain-code between derivation iterations (evidence: test_bip32 P2-CT-001). 2026-05-21: P2-SEC-002 batch_verify CSPRNG-seeded weights (schnorr_batch_verify_impl now XORs 32 CSPRNG bytes into batch_seed before weight derivation — prevents Wagner-style adversary from pre-computing weights); P2-CT-RT-004 adaptor_nonce fixed 2-iter CT select (data-dependent retry loop removed, ct::scalar_select applied). Prior: frost_keygen_finalize receives_shares erased via RAII (TASK-004); frost_aggregate R.x zeros check (TASK-003); frost_lagrange_coefficient CT arithmetic (P1-002); schnorr_adaptor_extract rejects sig.s==0 (SEC-007); pn_unpack_points parse return checked (SEC-003); musig2 zero-tweak rejection (SEC-005); ellswift_create_fast CT path (CT-003); shim_schnorr msglen forwarded (SHIM-004). See SECURITY_CLAIMS.md.

preconditions: seckey is scalar-valid and non-zero
message buffer length matches API contract
postconditions: signature verifies under corresponding pubkey
secret-dependent control flow remains constant-time

preconditions: private key is valid scalar
digest input is exactly 32 bytes
postconditions: output signature is DER/P1363 conformant per API
nonce path does not branch on secret data

preconditions: public key is parseable and curve-valid
signature encoding matches selected API mode
postconditions: accepts only mathematically valid signatures
rejects malformed or non-canonical encodings

preconditions: x-only pubkey must be valid and canonical
signature length must match BIP340 requirements
postconditions: accepts only valid BIP340 signatures
rejects malformed x-only parity/confusion cases

preconditions: peer public key is strict-validated on curve
private key scalar is valid and non-zero
postconditions: shared secret is deterministic for input pair
invalid-curve and twist inputs are rejected

preconditions: path grammar is valid
depth and index constraints are within policy
postconditions: derived key path respects hardened/non-hardened rules
out-of-range path elements are rejected

preconditions: all participant keys are valid points
key list canonicalization rules are enforced
postconditions: aggregate key is deterministic for signer set
ordering manipulation does not alter valid result

preconditions: participant index is valid and unique in session
binding factors are computed from canonical transcript
postconditions: partial signatures bind to transcript and signer set
secret share and nonce handling stays CT-safe

preconditions: batch entries reference valid pubkeys/messages/signatures
batch size is within implementation bounds
postconditions: accept/reject result matches single-verify semantics
identify-invalid path isolates bad entries

preconditions: backend context is initialized
host/device buffers satisfy shape contracts
postconditions: GPU verification semantics match CPU reference
backend mismatch or unsupported mode returns explicit error

preconditions: GPU context is initialized (CUDA, OpenCL, or Metal)
scan private key is a valid, non-zero scalar
spend_pubkeys33 is n_spend x 33 compressed pubkeys and tweak_pubkeys33 is n_tweaks x 33 compressed pubkeys
postconditions: spend-key-independent per-tweak work (ECDH scalar mul, BIP-352 tagged hash, hash x G) runs exactly once per tweak regardless of n_spend; only a mixed point add repeats per spend-key candidate
output is a row-major n_tweaks x n_spend matrix; with n_spend == 1 it is byte-identical to ufsecp_gpu_bip352_scan_batch's output
n_tweaks == 0 or n_spend == 0 is a valid no-op (UFSECP_OK, no output written)
an invalid or off-curve tweak or spend pubkey yields prefix64 == 0 for the affected row/column rather than failing the whole batch
failure_modes: invalid scan private key returns UFSECP_ERR_BAD_KEY
malformed pubkey, or n_tweaks/n_spend/their product too large, returns UFSECP_ERR_BAD_INPUT
